---
name: MVP User Story
about: Capture a single user story that belongs to the MVP milestone
title: "[MVP] <short description>"
labels: ["MVP", "user‑story"]
assignee: ""
milestone: MVP
---

## User story

**As a** `<role>`  
**I want** `<goal>`  
**so that** `<benefit>`

*(If you already have an issue number for this story, add `Related issue: #<num>` here – GitHub will turn it into a link.)*

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Criterion 1
- [ ] Criterion 2
- [ ] Criterion 3
- … *(add as many as needed)*

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Code written & reviewed
- [ ] Unit / integration tests added & passing
- [ ] Documentation updated (README / docs/USER_STORIES.md)
- [ ] Feature flag / config set for MVP release
- [ ] Deployed to staging and verified

## Additional context (optional)

- Links to designs, mockups, or related discussions  
- Dependencies on other stories (e.g., “Blocks #45”)  
- Risks / open questions  

---

*When you create the issue, GitHub will automatically apply the **MVP** label and attach it to the **MVP** milestone (if that milestone exists). You can edit the milestone name in the front‑matter (`milestone: MVP`) to match exactly what you have in your repo.*
